questions.question_text: How many classroom hours of pre-license education does Arkansas require for a salesperson?
questions.explanation: Arkansas requires 60 classroom hours of pre-licensing education, with at least 30 of those hours in basic principles of real estate.

questions.question_text: Of the 60 pre-license hours required in Arkansas, how many must cover basic principles of real estate?
questions.explanation: At least 30 of the 60 required pre-licensing hours must be in basic principles of real estate. The remaining 30 hours can be in other approved real estate courses.

questions.question_text: A 17-year-old completes all 60 hours of pre-license education. Can this person take the salesperson exam?
questions.correct_answer: No, applicants must be at least 18
distractors: Yes, if a parent co-signs the application
Yes, at any age after completing education
questions.explanation: Arkansas requires salesperson applicants to be at least 18 years old (the age of majority). Completing education alone does not waive the age requirement.

questions.question_text: An applicant completed Arkansas pre-license education three years ago but never took the exam. Is the education certificate still valid?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, salesperson certificates do not expire
distractors: Only if the applicant completes a refresher
questions.explanation: In Arkansas, salesperson education certificates do NOT expire. This differs from broker education certificates, which are valid for only 36 months.

questions.question_text: In addition to education, what else must a salesperson applicant complete before licensure in Arkansas?
questions.explanation: Arkansas salesperson applicants must pass both state and federal background checks in addition to completing 60 hours of pre-license education and passing the exam.

questions.question_text: How many hours of post-license education must a new Arkansas salesperson complete?
questions.explanation: New Arkansas salespersons must complete 18 hours of post-license education within 6 months of initial licensure through the AREC post-licensure course.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must an Arkansas licensee complete each year?
questions.explanation: All Arkansas licensees must complete 7 hours of CE annually on a calendar year cycle: 1-hour safety course + 3-hour required course + 3-hour elective.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is part of the required Arkansas CE curriculum?
questions.explanation: Arkansas CE includes a 1-hour Required Safety Course, a 3-hour Required Course, and a 3-hour elective, totaling 7 hours annually.

questions.question_text: A salesperson obtains their license on September 1. By when must they complete post-license education?
questions.correct_answer: By the end of March of the following year
distractors: By December 31 of the same year
By September 1 of the following year
questions.explanation: Post-license education documentation is due no later than the end of the month six months after initial licensure. For a September 1 license that is March 31 of the following year. Missing the deadline places the license on inactive status.

questions.question_text: A Principal Broker's CE requirements differ from a salesperson's because the broker must take:
distractors: A course approved by the Governor
questions.explanation: Principal Brokers and Executive Brokers must take the safety course, a broker supervision/agency course, and an elective — still 7 hours total, but with a broker-specific required course instead of the standard required course.

questions.question_text: Which activity requires a real estate license in Arkansas?
questions.correct_answer: Listing property for sale for compensation
distractors: An owner selling their own home
An attorney closing a real estate transaction
questions.explanation: Listing, selling, buying, exchanging, or leasing real estate for compensation requires a license. Owners, attorneys acting within their practice, and court-appointed individuals are exempt.

questions.question_text: A property owner hires their nephew to collect rent from tenants. The nephew lives off-site and receives monthly payment. Does the nephew need a license?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, collecting rent for compensation requires one
distractors: No, family members are always exempt
No, if managing fewer than 5 units
Only if the nephew also negotiates leases
questions.explanation: Collecting rent for compensation is a licensed activity. The on-site residential manager exemption only applies to managers who reside at the property as part of their employment.

questions.question_text: Which of the following persons is exempt from Arkansas real estate licensing?
questions.correct_answer: An on-site residential manager living at the property
distractors: A paid referral agent at a brokerage
An investor who lists rentals for others
A consultant who negotiates sales for a fee
questions.explanation: On-site residential managers who reside at the property and manage as part of their employment are exempt. Property owners, attorneys, and court-appointed individuals are also exempt.

questions.question_text: Which Arkansas license status lets an individual hold a broker's license while working under a principal broker WITHOUT supervisory authority over other licensees?
questions.explanation: An associate broker holds a broker's license but works under a principal broker, as an employee or independent contractor, and has no supervisory authority over any other licensee. Executive brokers, by contrast, may supervise other licensees.

questions.question_text: An attorney negotiates a real estate sale as part of their law practice. Must they hold a real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: No, attorneys are exempt within their practice
distractors: Yes, all negotiators need a license
Only if they collect a separate commission
questions.explanation: Attorneys acting within the scope of their legal practice are exempt from Arkansas real estate licensing requirements. This is one of the statutory exemptions.
